heroHeadline: Day 60: ACLED 'After El Mencho' Webinar Held — International Analysts Assess Fractura Total; Cobra Cell Dismantled in Colima
heroSubtitle: Day 60 since El Mencho's killing at Tapalpa, Jalisco (February 22, 2026). ACLED hosted its landmark 'After El Mencho: CJNG Succession Dynamics and the Future of Security in Mexico' webinar today at 10:00 a.m. Mexico City time — the first major international analytical examination of post-Mencho CJNG dynamics. Speakers Sandra Pellegrini (ACLED), Chris Dalby and Victoria Dittmar (InSight Crime) examined four dimensions: succession dynamics, criminal economy evolution, internal cohesion, and FIFA World Cup 2026 implications (49 days to June 11 opening match). FRACTURA TOTAL DAY 12 (declared April 11): The El Jardinero/El Sapo vs. O3/Los Cuinis internal war remains in a sustained dual-buildup phase — no direct inter-faction combat confirmed in the Jalisco heartland through Day 12. El Jardinero's multi-state plaza network (Jalisco, Nayarit, Zacatecas, Michoacán, Guerrero) and 'Monstruos' armored vehicle deployments on the Jalisco-Zacatecas border continue signaling pre-combat positioning. O3 retains Grupo Élite paramilitary command and Los Cuinis financial infrastructure. COLIMA COBRA CELL DISMANTLED (April 15-21): Mexican authorities dismantled the CJNG 'Cobra' cell in Colima state — 3 arrested (including 'El 51,' linked to 7 murders in two weeks) and 3 killed in prior confrontations. The cell operated on behalf of El Sapo's Port of Manzanillo faction using a rotating six-person outsider model. This is the third major enforcement action against El Sapo's Colima corridor in one week (following: 8 Manzanillo police arrested for CJNG intel leaks Apr 16; 3 CJNG operatives killed on Colima-Manzanillo highway Apr 17). WORLD CUP SECURITY (49 days): President Sheinbaum's 100,000-person security deployment remains in place across Mexico City, Guadalajara, and Monterrey, supplemented by an April 22 National Guard surge at all major archaeological sites following the non-cartel Teotihuacán shooting (April 20). García Harfuch confirmed the tourist security expansion is additive, not replacing, the anti-CJNG posture. ACLED webinar recording will be posted on YouTube with English subtitles. Operativo Enjambre cumulative total: 80+ arrests. El 85 sentencing: July 31, 2026.
footerNote: Data sourced from SEDENA, SSPC, FGR, DEA, U.S. DOJ, NCTC, U.S. Treasury (OFAC), InSight Crime, Crisis Group, Wilson Center, Reuters, AP, NPR, Wall Street Journal, El País, Washington Post, Al Jazeera, South China Morning Post, CGTN, France 24, Latin Times, Infobae México, La Silla Rota, WOLA, Milenio, Excélsior, Proceso, El Financiero, El Imparcial, Borderland Beat, RED Michoacán, Semanario ZETA, La Voz de la Frontera, TV Azteca Baja California, Time, CNN, ACLED, OCCRP, El Heraldo de México, and major Mexican news outlets. All contested figures noted. Last update: April 23, 2026.
